title: Bodum Bistro Electric Blade Coffee Grinder, Black
description:
About this item
COFFEE GRINDER: BISTRO Blade coffee grinders take whole coffee beans from coarse to fine quickly and efficiently; fresh brewing from the comfort of your home
BLADE & MOTOR: The blade grinder has a strong, durable stainless steel cutting blade that spins at a precise RPM, plus a powerful 150W motor
SLEEK DESIGN: Push-button control allows for pulse action or continuous grind, and transparent lid allows the fineness grade to be observed to ensure coffee is made to your liking
COMPACT: Small coffee grinder has convenient cord storage in the base of grinder, perfect if you plan to leave out on the kitchen counter
SERVINGS: This powerful coffee grinder makes enough coffee needed for brewing 8 cups of French press; pair with Bodum coffee maker
reviews_5s:
Easy to use, does the job and great price!I purchased this coffee grinder because of its light weight, the grippy rubber outer covering, and the fact that I've been happy with other Bodum grinders I've owned. When my arthritis is acting up, the weight and grippy attributes are just what the doctor ordered.

I saw the review remarks about the short (six-inch) cord, but figured I could live with that, given the weight and grip benefits.

The reality is that, when the grinder comes out of the box, it does have about six inches of cord emerging from the back of the grinder.

The further reality is that, once the *rest* of the cord is unwound from the cord storage area in the grinder base, it turns out to be about 35-36" long.

So while the cord storage area obviously does an excellent job of hiding what might be "excess cord" for one user, it also allows those so inclined to "let it all hang out" if they wish.

Beyond that, this whirly-blade grinder does its primary coffee grinding job at least as well as or better than the (many) other blade grinders that I've owned or tried. In my opinion, it is faster, more efficient and quieter than most of the other w-blade grinders on the market, including the earlier Bodum C-Mill metal and plastic models that I've owned.

Update: After using this grinder more, I'm even more impressed with it. I load it up with 52-53 grams of coffee beans at a time (close to a full hopper), and it produces a nice, even drip grind in 30 seconds. The coffee is ground much more evenly than with other blade grinders I've used, and in less time. The key to this seems to be that a vortex is immediately formed in the grinding chamber, getting the beans moving quickly through the blades, and keeping the progressively finer ground coffee moving. Darker roast (oily) beans may start to cake up along the sides after 15-20 seconds, but a gentle shake or rocking motion keeps them moving through the process until the desired grind size is reached. I'm glad I bought this grinder, and would recommend it to anyone.I purchased this to replace the coffee grinder that I had for almost two decades. My old one finally died one morning and I needed one fast. Amazon had a lot of grinders and I went with this one because I also have a Bodum pour over set. This grinder has one speed: grind. Depending on whether you pulse or long press, the grind will vary. It's not very noisy and works well. The resulting ground coffee tastes great and is suitable for pourovers and coffee makers. Awesome value and price for less than $20. A brush to aid in cleaning this grinder would be nice to include with the purchase.Works well and easy to pack and travel for overnight fresh brew French pressed coffeeFor those having difficulty finding the "warranty certificate" that Bodum tells you, "the Seller must fill in at the time of purchase", take a look at the back cover of the User Manual. That cover constitutes the WARRANTY CERTIFICATE, at least in the USA.

Now, you'll have to keep a copy of your original Amazon Order by which you purchased the unit. (Not diffifult, right?) Make sure that the copy you print has the date of purchase on it and shows that you've paid for the unit by name and price. Ideally, the order will say "Shipped" at the top or something to that effect, but not "pending".

Fill in the date of purchase on the back cover of the User Manual, staple the print out of the order as above, and include a letter to Bodum asking for the unit to be serviced or replaced. Explain why you believe that the unit failed due to faulty design or manufacturing of the original.

Note that normal wear and tear and end-ser abuse or mishandling of the unit ARE NOT COVERED under the warranty. Neither will any unit be covered if the repair request is posted more than two years from the date of purchase.

If you wish to have a RMA, you'll have to contact Bodum directly by phone or email for that. Good luck !

Frankly, if your unit is more than 1 year old, toss it and buy a new one. It's not worth the trouble to have it returned and repaired, right ?It delivered what it is promised, very good and aesthetic look. It doesn’t get too hot (you don’t need to be using for more than 40 seconds even with the maximum amount and it’s easy to clean, overall we really likedNothing fancy, just a grinder. I messed around with it several times to figure out how I wanted my coffee beans ground and it has worked very well throughout. No issues, good size. The lid has to line up just right for the pulse button to work but after a few times it becomes simple.Be careful with buying from third-party sellers. My first order came used and broken. ( Maybe someone didn't check a return thoroughly and just sold it back to me. However, after I spoke to Amazon my replacement was brand new for sure. Works great!Grinder works as intended and is sturdy, looks good.Bodum did a great job here! This grinder looks great and works very well despite the low cost. It’s really easy to clean too, I just wipe it with a dry paper towel and it’s good to go.
reviews_1s:
It broke within only a couple of months, nothing was done to it except for regular grinding. Just stop working out of nowhere, thought I contact their support, hoping maybe I can get a replacement or help but no even a response. Even if it was a no, would have loved to received a response. So yeah I don't recommend this one nor this brand, stops working for no reach and no customer help/support.I have owned the same model Bodum for probably 10 years. I could tell it was on it's last legs so I went ahead and bought another one because it has been great and very good quality. I just used this one from Amazon for the first time after my old one broke and it is terrible. The entire thing is made of cheaper plastic than before. The outside part that was easier to grip before is a very hard plastic. The top is very loose unlike the previous version. Not surprisingly, coffee grounds are all over my counter top after my first grind due to the loose top. Sorry Bodum. I will never buy one of your products again.This product worked great for 2 months and then we went to grind beans one day and the blades wouldn’t move, the grinder got super hot and it started to smell like burned plastic. Really disappointed since Bodum is a reputable brand with great products. I have other Bodum products and love them. Very disappointing.Won’t turn on after a couple uses :/ so disappointed!This thing is terrible. It lasted a month before it stopped grinding the beans. It never really grounded them fully which was ok since I like them a bit on the chunkier side but now it grinds them at best in half. Now my coffee is clear light brown since the beans aren’t ground. Not the best way to start your morning. I can’t return it at this point so in the trash it goes.The lid fits the grinder poorly, very loosely. It doesn’t seat. Which means it makes contact to turn it on only after many, frustrating attempts. I’m sorry to say I hate it so basically don’t use it.I have had a Bodum coffee grinder for over 10yrs and it worked great with no issues. I accidentally dropped it and it broke.
So I bought a new one to replace it. Big Mistake! This one arrived and I could tell instantly that it was not the same quality as my first one. The plastic felt different, the lid didn't fit as snug. The button was super flimsy and guess what after 2 months it stopped working, it's like the button wires inside the unit weren't making contact.
Just bad.I am totally changing my original review which said this grinder was excellent other than you need to clear the coffee dust out of the engagement latch from time to time with compressed air. That was a mistake. The real reason this grinder dies after a month or less of daily use is the internal components are ridiculously cheap and fragile. I really didn't care about getting a refund, I was more interested in knowing the real reason this grinder fails, so I took it apart.

The photo shows the micro switch which I harvested out of the unit. With this switch removed, I can hard wire the power and the motor will run, no problem. This switch is faulty, it is cheaply made in China, it costs about 19 cents for a manufacture to buy. I cannot get current to run through this switch. I guess Bodum wants to play the numbers game here and sell multitudes of grinders hoping people will only use them occasionally. They know these parts won't stand up to daily use. And for me to replace this switch, I cannot get it for .19 cents unless I buy in bulk of 200 switches. I have to pay closer to $20 on ebay for a compatible replacement switch, which is equivalent to the price of the grinder.

Cheap grinder with cheap parts, confirmed! not reliable for other than light occasional use.I wanted to have a better option for grinding coffee than the little blender I was using. Even though this was pretty expensive for something as simple as a coffee grinder, I thought It would be good quality, especially because of the good reviews. Once I got it, I noticed that it ground the beans very unevenly unless I grounded the coffee to pretty much powder, but I prefer my coffee more coarsely ground so it doesn't go through the filter, but when doing this, it left some beans completely uncracked, while others were finely ground. I thought at first that maybe this is just a consequence of using a blade coffee grinder and not a burr coffee grinder, but regardless, two weeks later, it just stopped working for no reason. I didn't do anything to it, didn't expose it to water, didn't drop it, it just stopped working. I suggest looking elsewhere for a coffee grinder.

Edit: It somehow started working again, I don't know what was wrong with it. The button you press to make it grind has a little tab it presses down, and that gets filled up with ground coffee almost every time you use it. The lid isn't secure, so as your grinding the ground coffee spills down the edges of the grinder and makes a huge mess. This might be the worst electric grinder on the market.I gave this to my son as a gift. After a month it stopped working. We both contacted the manufacturer’s customer service several times. After sending a requested video of the product not working, and replying to all the questions they had, they stopped responding to our emails. Now we are left with a broken product that cannot be returned.
